text: This paper examines the formal and technical conditions for utilizing calcareous fly ash as an active pozzolanic-hydraulic agent in cements and binders. The study is supported by experimental results from hydraulic road binders and masonry cements formulated with calcareous fly ash sourced from the Bełchatów Power Station. Various mixtures were evaluated, including binders with varying concentrations of calcareous fly ash, as well as blended systems incorporating multiple combustion by-products, such as siliceous fly ash and fluidized bed combustion (FBC) fly ash. The research focused on assessing normative properties essential for hydraulic road binders and masonry cements. The findings demonstrate that calcareous fly ash from Bełchatów is a highly effective constituent, capable of producing high-quality hydraulic binders and cements that meet current industry standards and technical approvals.
